reasoning: In May 2025, the Commerce Department rescinded the Biden-era AI Diffusion Rule before its compliance date. While the DOC press release stated an intent to issue a replacement rule to "strengthen export controls on semiconductors worldwide," searches of the Federal Register show no such replacement rule published between May and late October 2025. Given it is already late October, the window for a new rule to be drafted, published, and reach its effective date by December 31, 2025, is narrowing rapidly. Standard rulemaking timelines are lengthy, even if Interim Final Rules are sometimes used. Additionally, any new rule must meet the criteria of being a broad expansion rather than just targeted sanctions. Thus, the probability is slightly under the recent crowd aggregate reflecting the passing of time and shrinking window for the year.
